data.narrative:
Isar Aerospace is a Series C-stage space and aerospace company that has raised over $400M and carries a current post-money valuation exceeding $1.0B. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 4 same-stage private peers in Space & Aerospace (Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Varda Space Industries, and Skyryse) plus 5 public market comps including Rocket Lab (RKLB), Planet Labs (PL), and Axiom Space Technologies (ASTS).

Two triangulation methods apply to Isar. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier from the $1.0B Series C base, yielding $1.7B. Peer-median analysis examines the 4 same-sector, same-stage private comps, which range from $1.1B (Skyryse) to $2.5B (Axiom Space), with a median of $1.6B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied due to the absence of target revenue disclosure, typical for private companies. 

Triangulating the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $2.0B (high), with a central estimate of $1.7B. This range reflects tightness between methodologies, though material dispersion exists at the peer level. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, competitive differentiation within launch services, and capital efficiency relative to peers—factors not directly embedded in stage and peer benchmarks alone.
